title: HUMIC ACID
description: Humic acid is a complex organic compound derived from the decomposition of plant and animal matter through a process called humification. It exhibits antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, chelating, and adsorption properties, working by binding to toxins and heavy metals while modulating immune responses. Primarily used for detoxification, gastrointestinal support, and as an antiviral and anti-inflammatory agent.

research.title: Therapeutic Efficiency of Humic Acids in Intoxications
research.summary: Review demonstrating humic acids' effectiveness in detoxification through chelating properties, ability to bind heavy metals and mycotoxins, and antioxidant capacity in treating various intoxications.

research.title: Clinical review of humic acid as an antiviral: Leadup to translational applications in clinical humeomics
research.summary: Clinical review establishing humic substances' antiviral properties and potential therapeutic applications, introducing the field of clinical humeomics for healthcare providers.

research.title: The Antiinflammatory Properties of Humic Substances: A Mini Review
research.summary: Mini review examining the anti-inflammatory mechanisms of humic substances and their therapeutic potential in inflammatory conditions.

sentiment.summary: Limited user experience data available for comprehensive sentiment analysis. Current evidence suggests potential benefits but lacks substantial user feedback to establish clear consensus on effectiveness and practical use.

effects.label: Heavy Metal Chelation:
effects.description: Strong evidence for binding and removing heavy metals from the body through polyelectrolyte properties

effects.label: Gastrointestinal Support:
effects.description: Effective for treating digestive tract conditions and binding toxins in the gut

effects.label: Immune Modulation:
effects.description: Shows promise in modulating immune responses and supporting overall immunity

effects.label: Broad Therapeutic Range:
effects.description: Applications span from detoxification to anti-inflammatory and antiviral uses

dosage.label: Variable Dosing:
dosage.description: Dosage depends on source, concentration, and intended use; no standardized human dosing established

dosage.label: Product-Specific:
dosage.description: Different formulations (liquid, powder, capsule) require manufacturer-specific dosing guidelines

dosage.label: Veterinary Use:
dosage.description: Well-established dosing protocols exist for livestock and animal applications

dosage.label: Clinical Context:
dosage.description: Therapeutic dosing should be determined based on specific condition being treated

sideEffects.label: Generally Well-Tolerated:
sideEffects.description: Research indicates humic acids are generally safe with minimal reported adverse effects

sideEffects.label: Gastrointestinal Effects:
sideEffects.description: Potential for mild digestive changes when first starting supplementation

sideEffects.label: Quality Concerns:
sideEffects.description: Variability in product purity and composition may affect safety and efficacy

sideEffects.label: Limited Long-term Data:
sideEffects.description: Insufficient human research on extended use and potential long-term effects

availability.label: Widely Available:
availability.description: Found in various forms including supplements, soil amendments, and agricultural products

availability.label: Quality Variation:
availability.description: Significant differences in purity, molecular weight, and composition between sources and brands

availability.label: Regulatory Status:
availability.description: Generally considered safe for agricultural and veterinary use; human supplement use less regulated
